halts.summary: lake build cannot proceed because the mathlib cache (C:\Users\shkub\.cache\mathlib\) is in a half-populated state: 8399/8399 .ltar artefacts downloaded but 7801/8399 decompressions failed with Windows 'Access is denied. (os error 5)'. As a consequence, 'lake build --no-build' reports the canonical witnesses Mathlib.Lean.Meta.RefinedDiscrTree.Basic / Mathlib.Util.AtomM / Mathlib.Logic.UnivLE (and presumably many more) as 'target is out-of-date and needs to be rebuilt'. Continuing to a real 'lake build' would trigger a from-source rebuild of ~7800 mathlib modules at v4.30.0-rc2 (estimated wallclock measured in many hours and likely failing on disk/memory pressure during the Mathlib monolith compile).

remediation_options.description: Operator manually frees the locked .ltar files (close VS Code Lean extension, OneDrive sync client, Windows Defender real-time scan on .cache\mathlib, possibly elevated PowerShell), then re-runs 'lake update mathlib' (or 'lake exe cache get!'). Expected single-shot fix if the lock source is OneDrive/AV.
remediation_options.estimated_wallclock_minutes: 60-120 (download already cached on disk; only decompression needs to succeed)

remediation_options.description: Delete C:\Users\shkub\.cache\mathlib\*.ltar entirely, then re-run 'lake exe cache get!'. Costs the full ~5GB re-download but eliminates the locked-file state.
remediation_options.risk: LOW (no source-tree touch; .cache is by definition reproducible)

remediation_options.description: Operator-tier: revert lean/lean-toolchain from leanprover/lean4:v4.30.0-rc2 back to the v4.30.0-rc1 (or older) pin that had a working cache locally. NOT RECOMMENDED -- the bump was per slot 148.B and is the predicate condition for the WallisFamily.lean L3 import substitute (Mathlib.Analysis.Asymptotics.Defs only exists at the new mathlib pin).
remediation_options.risk: HIGH (regresses slot 148.B AND slot 148.A import-substitute work)

remediation_options.description: Accept that the Lean axis is blocked at the toolchain/cache layer pending operator-tier intervention, and recategorise slot-148-E-L282-unfold-fix from 'in_progress' to 'BLOCKED_ON_TOOLCHAIN_LAYER' in SQL todo, with a forward pointer to the OP-Z lift directive packet. Allows downstream T1 / T2 work on the F1-F8 axes to proceed without waiting on Lean.
remediation_options.risk: LOW (status-only; defers but does not regress)

halts.recommended_remediation: OPT_X1_CACHE_REPAIR_PRIVILEGED first; if that fails after one attempt, fall back to OPT_X2_FRESH_CACHE_DIR. OPT_X3 explicitly NOT recommended. OPT_X4 acceptable if X1+X2 both fail.

lean_working_tree_state_at_halt.lean/WallisFamily.lean: M (slot 148.A import-substitute + slot 148.E L284 simp tactic; not yet operator-committed)
lean_working_tree_state_at_halt.lean/lean-toolchain: M (slot 148.B bump to v4.30.0-rc2; not yet operator-committed)
lean_working_tree_state_at_halt.lean/lake-manifest.json: ?? (rewritten by Phase A.1 lake update with new mathlib rev 14b67616...; not yet operator-committed)
